rule: Tool slug must be unique, lowercase, stable, and URL-safe.
implication_for_datalens_backlog: Do not mirror raw marketplace titles. Normalize every backlog item into a canonical slug and map duplicates to the same slug.

rule: tools.manifest.json entries require slug, title, category, actions, seoTitle, seoDescription, and exampleInput.
implication_for_datalens_backlog: Every DataLens family needs a ToolNexus-ready metadata record before UI or executor work starts.

rule: A ToolShell page expects a tool manifest JSON, runtime JS module, and HTML template under the governed ToolNexus paths.
implication_for_datalens_backlog: For external APIs, create lightweight ToolShell assets that collect input, call the execution API, and render normalized results.

rule: Each tool needs an IToolExecutor implementation with a Slug and SupportedActions aligned to the manifest.
implication_for_datalens_backlog: Add a reusable DataLens proxy executor or generated executor per slug instead of wiring UI directly to external APIs.

rule: ToolNexus execute requests are sent as { input: <json>, options: { key: value } }.
implication_for_datalens_backlog: Design DataLens endpoints so ToolNexus can pass the entire input object into the endpoint body with minimal reshaping.

rule: ToolNexus expects execution output as a string plus success, error, notFound, insight, and runtime identity metadata.
implication_for_datalens_backlog: Wrap DataLens JSON responses as serialized output strings or add a dedicated ToolNexus adapter that extracts the normalized payload.

rule: ToolNexus defaults to 15s timeout, 512KB payload, anonymous=false, concurrency=16, and validates blueprint policy fields.
implication_for_datalens_backlog: Mark public low-risk tools anonymous only after review. Set stricter per-tool policy for heavy scrapers and large-input tools.

rule: Tool blueprint validation caps timeout at 120s, max requests per minute at 600, and max input size at 5,000,000 bytes.
implication_for_datalens_backlog: Any DataLens contract that needs more than these values should be async or private, not a direct public ToolNexus tool.

rule: Manifest and executor first, UI after shell contract passes.
implication_for_datalens_backlog: For import work: finalize canonical slug, actions, contract, and policy before spending time on ToolShell polish.

rule: Public tool pages and example coverage are validated as part of ToolNexus governance.
implication_for_datalens_backlog: Each family needs a docs page with plain-language examples, SEO copy, FAQs, and supported actions.

rule: Use ToolNexus executor as the public boundary and let that executor call DataLens APIs or internal workers.
implication_for_datalens_backlog: Do not embed DataLens endpoint URLs directly in page JavaScript. Keep routing, retries, auth, and policy enforcement server-side.

rule: Public catalog tools should avoid abuse-oriented capabilities even if technically possible.
current_toolnexus_source: Derived from ToolNexus policy model and public catalog governance expectations
implication_for_datalens_backlog: Traffic generators, fake engagement, and manipulative view tools should be rejected from public ToolNexus onboarding.
